name: Problem Manager
description: Reduces the likelihood and impact of incidents by identifying actual and potential causes, managing workarounds and known errors.

triggers.description: Activates when user mentions problems, root cause analysis, known errors, or recurring patterns
problem-kedb-review — Reviews KEDB for stale or outdated entries every Thursday at 09:00
incident-to-problem-promotion — Analyzes recurring incidents for problem promotion every 2 hours

actions.name: manage-problem-record
actions.description: Create, update, query, or close problem records

inputs.name: action
inputs.description: Operation: create, update, query, or close

inputs.name: details
inputs.description: Problem details (category, priority, description, related incidents)

outputs.name: problemRecord
outputs.description: The problem record with current state

actions.name: manage-kedb
actions.description: Create, query, and maintain known error records and workarounds

inputs.name: details
inputs.description: Known error details and workaround instructions

actions.name: correlate-incidents
actions.description: Analyze incident patterns and identify recurring issues

actions.name: run-rca
actions.description: Execute structured root cause analysis using 5 Whys, Ishikawa, Fault Tree, or Kepner-Tregoe templates

outputs.name: rcaResult
outputs.description: Root cause analysis findings and conclusions

actions.name: query-ci-dependencies
actions.description: Query CI relationships and service dependencies for impact analysis

outputs.name: dependencies
outputs.description: CI dependency map and service impact

actions.name: view-trend-analysis
actions.description: Visualize incident trends, frequencies, and patterns

inputs.name: groupBy
inputs.description: Group by: category, CI, service, priority

actions.name: raise-rfc
actions.description: Raise RFCs for permanent problem resolution via the Change Request Interface

crossPracticeIntegrations.description: Recurring incidents trigger problem investigation; workarounds feed back to incident resolution
Problem resolution drives RFCs; failed changes may create new problems
Problem impact assessment considers SLA implications
CMDB provides CI context for RCA; problem resolution may update CI records
RCA findings and workarounds published as knowledge articles
Vendor-related root causes require supplier engagement
Problem trends feed the CSI register and improvement initiatives

hitlControls.reason: Human must validate and approve identified root cause
Workarounds must be reviewed by SME before KEDB publication
Human approval required before raising change requests
Changing priority of critical problems requires human review
Human must confirm that permanent fix resolved the root cause
Auto-detected potential problems require human validation before investigation

knowledgeSources.description: Problem records, RCA findings, and resolution history
Incident patterns and trend data for root cause analysis
CI relationships and service dependency maps
